name: Parcae BPB optimization on RTX Pro 4500 Blackwell

description: Baseline: current code on SP1024, 300s, all new features off (MHC=none, EMA=0, SWA=0, POE=1). Much better than 1.762 noted in autoresearch.md — codebase has evolved.
Enable COMPILE_MODEL=1 on Blackwell. Steps jumped 1983→3123 (+57%), BPB improved 1.7065→1.6885 (-0.018). Compile gave massive throughput gain.

asi.hypothesis: torch.compile on Blackwell significantly improves step throughput, allowing more training in same wall-clock and thus better BPB

description: Stack COMPILE_MUON_BACKEND=1 on top of COMPILE_MODEL=1. BPB 1.6897 (slightly worse than 1.6885), steps 2998 < 3123. Muon backend compile adds overhead without benefit.

asi.hypothesis: COMPILE_MUON_BACKEND=1 adds overhead; worse steps and BPB than COMPILE_MODEL=1 alone
asi.next_action_hint: Keep COMPILE_MODEL=1, revert COMPILE_MUON_BACKEND. Next try other ideas like longer seq len or PoE experts.

description: TRAIN_SEQ_LEN=1024 (was 512) with COMPILE_MODEL=1. Massive BPB improvement: 1.688→1.660 (-0.028). Steps 2989 (comparable to seq512+compile's 3123). Longer seq length gives much better context utilization.

asi.hypothesis: Doubling seq len from 512→1024 on Blackwell with torch.compile gives dramatically better BPB via improved context learning per step, without significantly hurting throughput due to compile optimization

description: Scale MODEL_DIM and RECURRENT_DIM from 256→320. BPB 1.660→1.639 (-0.021). Steps 3063. Memory 625MB. Larger model capacity helps a lot with compile.

asi.hypothesis: With compile on Blackwell, larger model (320 vs 256) gives better quality per step without throughput penalty

description: Scale MODEL_DIM from 320→384. BPB 1.639→1.620 (-0.019). Steps 3048. Memory 737MB. Compile keeps throughput excellent even at larger sizes.

asi.hypothesis: Model scaling continues to yield BPB improvements on Blackwell with compile. Even dim=384 maintains ~3000 steps in 300s.

description: MODEL_DIM 384→448. Marginal BPB gain: 1.620→1.616 (-0.004). Steps 3001. Submission bytes 9.3MB (+2.1MB). Diminishing returns starting to show.

asi.hypothesis: Model scaling improvement is diminishing: 256→320 gave -0.021, 320→384 gave -0.019, 384→448 gave only -0.004. May be approaching optimal size/quality tradeoff for this budget.

description: N_LAYERS_IN_RECURRENT_BLOCK 2→3 with dim=384. BPB 1.620→1.613 (-0.007). Steps 2877 (-171). Submission bytes 8.5MB (+1.3MB). Modest gain, fewer steps due to larger compute per step.

asi.hypothesis: Adding recurrent depth helps BPB modestly but hurts steps and artifact size. Model width scaling gave better returns than depth scaling.

description: MLP_MULT 3→4 with dim=384, 3 recurrent layers. BPB 1.613→1.600 (-0.013). Steps 2756. Submission 9.9MB. Good quality gain from wider MLP.

asi.hypothesis: MLP_MULT=4 gives substantial BPB improvement over 3 in this config. Quality gain outweighs throughput loss.

description: MEAN_RECURRENCE 2→3. Marginal BPB 1.600→1.599 (-0.002). Steps dropped 2756→2439. Not worth the throughput loss.

asi.hypothesis: Recurrence 3 is barely better than 2 on this config, but costs 11% fewer steps
asi.next_action_hint: Revert to recurrence=2. Try other axes like QK norm variations, RoPE dims, or gradient clipping.
asi.rollback_reason: BPB gain 0.002 is marginal vs 11% fewer steps

description: ROPE_DIMS 16→32 with seq1024. Marginal BPB 1.600→1.598 (-0.002). Steps 2693 (only -63). Small but clean improvement.

asi.hypothesis: RoPE 32 slightly better than 16 for seq1024 context, yielding marginal BPB improvement

description: N_LAYERS_IN_CODA 1→2. BPB 1.598→1.592 (-0.006). Steps 2523 (-170). Submission 11.4MB. Adding a second coda layer gives substantial quality improvement.

asi.hypothesis: Second coda layer provides substantial quality gain in this architecture. Bottleneck may be moving from recurrent to coda depth.

description: GRAD_CLIP_NORM=0.3. Marginal BPB 1.592→1.591 (-0.001). Steps 2529. Very small improvement, near noise floor.

asi.hypothesis: Gradient clipping 0.3 gives marginal (possibly noise) improvement. Worth keeping since it's cheap and safe
asi.next_action_hint: Try other axes: more warmup, different LR, QK norm off, bigger bigram buckets, or smaller seq to test tradeoffs

description: BIGRAM_HASH_BUCKETS 4096→8192. BPB 1.591→1.589 (-0.002). Steps 2469 (-60). Submission 12.4MB. Small but consistent improvement.

asi.hypothesis: Bigger bigram hash table helps slightly with more capacity for n-gram patterns

description: MLP_MULT 4→5. BPB 1.589→1.588 (-0.001). Steps 2407 (-62). Submission 13.9MB. Very marginal gain vs cost.

asi.hypothesis: MLP_MULT=5 barely improves over 4 despite much larger artifact. Diminishing returns from MLP scaling.
asi.next_action_hint: Revert MLP_MULT to 4 or try 3.5. Instead try increasing model dim further (448 already tried, marginal). Or try training longer with more batch tokens per step.

description: TRAIN_BATCH_TOKENS 16384→32768. DRAMATIC BPB 1.588→1.506 (-0.082). Steps 2480. Processing 81M tokens vs 41M in 300s. Compile enables much higher throughput, doubling batch size doubles tokens processed.

asi.hypothesis: Doubling batch tokens doubles total tokens trained in 300s (81M vs 41M), massively improving BPB. torch.compile on Blackwell enables large batch sizes without proportional slowdown.

description: TRAIN_BATCH_TOKENS 32768→65536. BPB 1.506→1.440 (-0.066). Steps 2315. Step time only increased 7% (121→130ms) for 2× batch. Compile enables massive throughput.

asi.hypothesis: Blackwell compile makes large batch sizes nearly free per-token. Batch=65536 yields ~152M tokens in 300s. Massive BPB improvement from seeing more data.

description: TRAIN_BATCH_TOKENS 65536→81920. BPB 1.440→1.425 (-0.015). Steps 2207. Peak mem 1038MB. Still improving with larger batch, though gains slowing.

asi.hypothesis: Batch scaling continues to improve BPB. 81920 batch gives ~181M tokens in 300s. Compile amortizes batch overhead very well.

description: TRAIN_BATCH_TOKENS 81920→131072. BPB 1.425→1.415 (-0.010). Steps halved to 1476. Total tokens ~193M. Step time 203ms. Still improving but diminishing.

asi.hypothesis: Batch=131072 continues BPB improvement but step time increased significantly (203ms). Diminishing returns setting in. Total tokens ~193M.

description: TRAIN_BATCH_TOKENS 131072→114688. BPB 1.415→1.418 (worse). Batch 114688 not as good as 131072. Need to find optimal between 98304 and 131072.

asi.hypothesis: 114688 batch worse than 131072. Optimum likely near top of range. Try 122880 or 124928.
asi.next_action_hint: Try batch 122880 or 124928. Also try Scylla tokenizer with optimized config since compile enables massive throughput.

description: TRAIN_BATCH_TOKENS 122880. BPB 1.416 (vs 1.415 at 131072). Batch 131072 remains optimal in tested range.

asi.hypothesis: Batch 122880 gives BPB 1.416, slightly worse than 1.415 at 131072. 131072 seems to be the sweet spot in this range.
asi.next_action_hint: Stick with batch=131072. Now try Scylla tokenizer with optimized config (batch=131072, compile, dim=384).

description: SWA_ENABLED=1 with SWA_START_FRAC=0.2. BPB 1.415→1.414 (-0.001). Marginal but measurable improvement from weight averaging.

asi.hypothesis: SWA provides marginal BPB improvement (0.001). Worth keeping alongside large batch config.

description: SWA_START_FRAC 0.2→0.1. BPB 1.417 vs 1.414 at 0.2. Worse result, 0.2 is better.

asi.hypothesis: SWA start frac 0.2 is slightly better than 0.1
asi.next_action_hint: Keep SWA=0.2. Try other axes: longer warmup, different heads, or higher model dim with lower batch.

description: WARMUP_STEPS 500→300. BPB 1.416 (vs 1.414 at 500). Slightly worse.

asi.hypothesis: Warmup 300 gives slightly worse BPB than 500. 500 steps is appropriate for this large batch size.

description: Baseline validation: current code (HEAD 59de6d8, +1000 lines of default-off eval features) reproduces ~1.42 BPB on SP1024 with best config (dim=384, batch=131072, compile=1, SWA=1). Result 1.41995 vs historical best 1.41432 is within noise. All new features (sliding eval, PPM, ngram, TTT, DeepSeek MoE, LAuReL, QAT, GPTQ) are default-off. Validated code is ready for new experiments.

asi.hypothesis: Validate that default-off eval features don't regress training BPB
asi.features_added: sliding_window, ppm, ngram_eval, ttt, deepseek_moe, laurel, qat, gptq
asi.next_action_hint: Now experiment with PoE on SP1024 or Scylla with large batch

description: PoE on SP1024: POE_NUM_EXPERTS=3, POE_HEAD_LR=0.005. Marginal BPB improvement 1.41995→1.41688 (-0.003). Steps 1479→1436 (-3%), submission +0.7MB, memory +66MB. PoE is NOT the huge win on SP1024 that it was on Scylla. Need to try other POE_HEAD_LR values or expert counts.

asi.hypothesis: PoE=3 helps on Scylla but not on SP1024; marginal 0.003 BPB improvement
asi.next_action_hint: Try POE_NUM_EXPERTS=2 or different POE_HEAD_LR (0.002, 0.008). Or try Scylla with this config.

description: Double wall-clock to 600s (was 300s) with POE=3. MASSIVE BPB improvement 1.4169→1.3825 (-0.034). Steps 1436→2853 (+99%). Training saw ~373M tokens vs ~190M. The primary driver appears to be more training time, not PoE. Need to test POE=1 at 600s to disentangle. Also need to check if challenge target is 300s or 600s; RWKV docs say 10 min target.

asi.hypothesis: Doubling wall-clock from 300s to 600s doubles steps and massively improves BPB. POE=3 may or may not contribute.
asi.next_action_hint: Test POE=1 at 600s to isolate wall-clock vs PoE effect. Then try other axes at 600s.

description: POE=1 at 600s wall-clock: BPB 1.3834 (vs 1.3825 with POE=3 at 600s). Difference is 0.0009 — POE is not meaningful on SP1024. POE=1 has MORE steps (2945 vs 2853), SMALLER artifact (12.4MB vs 13.2MB), and LOWER memory. Keep POE=1. The real win is 600s wall-clock, not PoE. New best config: dim=384, batch=131k, compile=1, 600s, POE=1, SWA=1.

asi.hypothesis: POE does not help on SP1024 at any budget. The 600s wall-clock is the real breakthrough.
asi.conclusion: POE irrelevant on SP1024. Keep POE=1 for smaller artifact + more steps.
asi.next_action_hint: Revert POE=1, keep 600s. Next try model_dim=448 or more recurrent layers at 600s.

description: Strict-300s baseline on current HEAD (44c0581) with SWA=1: BPB 1.42747, 1389 steps, 12.6MB. Slightly worse than run 22's 1.41432 (same nominal config) likely from code drift (+1000 lines eval features). New baseline for feature-testing at strict 300s budget.

asi.hypothesis: Re-establish 300s baseline on current HEAD before feature ablations
asi.config: dim=384 batch=131k compile=1 SWA=1 POE=1 recur=2 core=3 coda=2 mlp=4
asi.notes: autoresearch.sh updated to use ${VAR:-default} so inline env var overrides work

description: EMA_ENABLED=1 decay=0.997 replacing SWA. BPB 1.43118 vs 1.42747 (worse by 0.004). Train loss lower (2.346 vs 2.358) but val worse — EMA with this decay is not a clear win at 300s/1400 steps. Effective window ~333 steps = last 24% of training.

asi.hypothesis: EMA with leaderboard-typical decay=0.997 replacing SWA
asi.rollback_reason: EMA decay=0.997 gave worse BPB than SWA baseline
asi.next_action_hint: Try EMA decay=0.999 (longer window) or EMA+SWA combo. Or try EMA with higher update_every.

description: EMA decay=0.999 badly underfits: BPB 1.6495 vs 1.4275 baseline. With only ~1400 training steps, decay=0.999 means EMA barely updates away from init. Effective window 1/(1-0.999)=1000 roughly equals total steps, so avg lags far behind converged weights.

asi.hypothesis: Longer EMA window via decay=0.999
asi.rollback_reason: EMA decay=0.999 too conservative for 1400-step regime
asi.next_action_hint: EMA is fundamentally mismatched to small-step regimes; stick with SWA. Try GPTQ, QAT, gradient checkpointing, or architecture features next.

description: GPTQ_ENABLED=1 on top of SWA baseline. BPB 1.42353 vs 1.42747 (-0.004). Small but clean win. Artifact size barely changed (12.57MB vs 12.56MB). GPTQ gives slightly better int8 quantization than naive per-tensor via blockwise Hessian-based quant.

asi.hypothesis: Post-training GPTQ improves int8 quantization accuracy vs naive zlib roundtrip
asi.notes: Training got 1340 steps (88s short of 300s cap because GPTQ eats ~12s reserve). Net win even with fewer steps.

description: QAT_BITS=6 QAT_START_STEP=500 without changing export bits. BPB 1.42713 vs 1.42353 GPTQ best (+0.004 worse). Artifact still int8+zlib (12.56MB) — QAT_BITS only affects fake-quant during training, not export. Without lowering QUANT_BITS to 6 the artifact stays the same size. Need to combine with QUANT_BITS=6 to realize size savings + enable larger model.

asi.hypothesis: QAT@6 alone with int8 export
asi.result: no-op improvement; QAT adapts weights for 6-bit but still exports as int8
asi.rollback_reason: QAT alone doesn't shrink artifact nor help BPB
asi.next_action_hint: Try QAT_BITS=6 QUANT_BITS=6 to actually export 6-bit and shrink artifact. Then scale MODEL_DIM up.
